title: anthriscus alpina
intro: Anthriscus alpina, also known as Alpine Chervil, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. The health benefits of Alpine Chervil include its ability to boost the immune system, improve digestion, and reduce stress. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and seeds are also used in cooking, adding a mild anise flavor to various dishes.
